sanskrit:
यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः।
आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते॥
transliteration:
yas tv ātma-ratir eva syād ātma-tṛptaś ca mānavaḥ |
ātmany eva ca santuṣṭas tasya kāryaṃ na vidyate ||
literal_meaning: One who delights in the self alone, is self-contented, a human being; satisfied only within the self—for him no duty is found.
contextual_meaning: When a person discovers an inner wellspring of joy that no longer depends on external validation or achievement, the compulsive sense of 'should' falls silent; action becomes spontaneous, not obligatory.
psychological_state: Autotelic flow-state generalized into daily life; intrinsic motivation replaces introjected 'musts'.
trigger_scenario: You finish a passion project at 2 a.m. and realize you no longer care if it wins awards—you coded, painted, or wrote simply because it felt alive.
anchor_text: When inner joy is complete, duty dissolves into effortless action.
